events.label: assistant
events.text: I’ll write the inspection conclusion directly to the required JSON file, treating the untooled mortar joints as an installed masonry defect with water-entry risk.

events.label: shell (exit 127)
events.text:
/bin/sh -lc "pwd && ls -la && cat > /app/answer.json <<'EOF'
{
  \"decision\": \"fail\",
  \"risk\": \"medium\",
  \"work_stage\": \"rough-complete\",
  \"component_conditions\": [\"installed-defective\", \"non-compliant\"],
  \"percent_complete\": 85,
  \"value\": 0,
  \"sound_source\": \"\",
  \"confidence\": 0.91,
  \"event_time\": 0,
  \"rate\": 0,
  \"order\": [],
  \"workable\": [],
  \"findings\": [
    \"Masonry bed and head joints are not tooled to a concave profile.\",
    \"Joint finish appears rough, open, or insufficiently compacted, creating water-entry paths.\",
    \"Installed masonry does not match the expected weather-resistant joint finish for exposed block or brick work.\"
  ],
  \"actions\": [
    \"Reject the affected masonry area pending correction.\",
    \"Rake out and repoint defective joints to the specified profile, or otherwise repair per approved submittals and TMS 602 requirements.\",
    \"Have repaired joints reinspected before closeout, cleaning, sealing, or covering adjacent work.\"
  ],
  \"rationale\": \"The observed condition is defective mortar joint finishing in installed block or brick masonry. Concave tooled joints compact the mortar and shed water; untooled or improperly finished joints are vulnerable to moisture entry and do not meet typical TMS 402/602 workmanship expectations for durable exterior masonry.\",
  \"references\": [
    \"TMS 402/602 masonry code and specification requirements for mortar joints, tooling, workmanship, and water-resistive performance\"
  ]
}
EOF
jq . /app/answer.json"
→ /app
total 8
drwxrwxrwx 1 root root 4096 Jul  5 06:10 .
drwxr-xr-x 1 root root 4096 Jul  6 22:33 ..

events.label: assistant
events.text: `jq` is not installed in this container, so I’m doing a lightweight JSON parse check with the available runtime instead of changing the file contents.
